evidence: Nevada GOED approved Google $600M Henderson data center Nov 15 2018; first public report Oct 11 2018 (Review-Journal); groundbreaking Jul 1 2019
Google announced additional $600M Henderson expansion on Sep 16 2020 doubling original investment to $1.2B; GOED abatement approved same date
Google announced $400M in Nevada data center enhancements (Henderson + Storey) in Aug 2024; Henderson-3 op. 2025 maps to this third-wave expansion; no dedicated press release found
Apple announced west campus expansion at Reno Technology Park Feb 14 2019 as part of $1B five-year U.S. data center investment; Reno 3 is among the west-campus buildings announced at that time
Same Feb 2019 Apple announcement of west campus expansion at Reno Technology Park; Reno 4 is a second building within same announcement wave; both opened 2019
Applied Blockchain (later Applied Digital) groundbreaking Sep 8 2022; GlobeNewswire press release Sep 19 2022; 180 MW crypto hosting facility; energized Mar 2023
Duplicate row in source data — same 180 MW Applied Blockchain/Applied Digital facility; same Sep 2022 groundbreaking announcement
Applied Digital broke ground on distinct 100 MW HPC facility at Ellendale campus Oct 2023; CoreWeave anchor tenant signed Mar 2024; building 1 reached 100 MW RFS Nov 2025

Oct 2019 Umatilla County Board of Commissioners approved 15-yr tax exemption for AWS fourth Umatilla campus; PDX63 built 2020 per baxtel; county approval is the earliest public signal for this campus wave
PDX130 on Wanapa/Powerline campus in Umatilla; same Oct 2019 Umatilla County tax exemption round covers the Umatilla campus expansion; no separate facility-level announcement found
PDX131 on Wanapa/Powerline campus in Umatilla; same Oct 2019 county approval covers the Umatilla expansion cluster; no separate facility-level announcement found
Hermiston City Council approved Amazon tax deal for new Lind/Penney campus data centers Sep 26 2022; PDX138 at Hermiston maps to this Sep 2022 approval round; op. 2024
